root_text.an4.114:1.1: “Catūhi, bhikkhave, aṅgehi samannāgato rañño nāgo rājāraho hoti rājabhoggo, rañño aṅganteva saṅkhaṁ gacchati.
root_text.an4.114:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo sotā ca hoti, hantā ca, khantā ca, gantā ca.
root_text.an4.114:2.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo sotā hoti?
root_text.an4.114:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo yamenaṁ hatthidammasārathi kāraṇaṁ kāreti—
root_text.an4.114:2.3: yadi vā katapubbaṁ yadi vā akatapubbaṁ—
root_text.an4.114:2.4: taṁ aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi katvā sabbacetasā samannāharitvā ohitasoto suṇāti.
root_text.an4.114:2.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo sotā hoti.
root_text.an4.114:3.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo hantā hoti?
root_text.an4.114:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo saṅgāmagato hatthimpi hanati, hatthāruhampi hanati, assampi hanati, assāruhampi hanati, rathampi hanati, rathikampi hanati, pattikampi hanati.
root_text.an4.114:3.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo hantā hoti.
root_text.an4.114:4.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo khantā hoti?
root_text.an4.114:4.2: Idha bhikkhave, rañño nāgo saṅgāmagato khamo hoti sattippahārānaṁ asippahārānaṁ usuppahārānaṁ pharasuppahārānaṁ bheripaṇavasaṅkhatiṇavaninnādasaddānaṁ.
root_text.an4.114:4.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo khantā hoti.
root_text.an4.114:5.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo gantā hoti?
root_text.an4.114:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo yamenaṁ hatthidammasārathi disaṁ peseti—
root_text.an4.114:5.3: yadi vā gatapubbaṁ yadi vā agatapubbaṁ—
root_text.an4.114:5.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo gantā hoti.
root_text.an4.114:5.6: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, catūhi aṅgehi samannāgato rañño nāgo rājāraho hoti rājabhoggo, rañño aṅganteva saṅkhaṁ gacchati.
root_text.an4.114:6.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu āhuneyyo hoti …pe… anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an4.114:6.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sotā ca hoti, hantā ca, khantā ca, gantā ca.
root_text.an4.114:7.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye desiyamāne aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi katvā sabbacetasā samannāharitvā ohitasoto dhammaṁ suṇāti.
root_text.an4.114:7.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sotā hoti.
root_text.an4.114:8.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu uppannaṁ kāmavitakkaṁ nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti hanati byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti, uppannaṁ byāpādavitakkaṁ …pe… uppannaṁ vihiṁsāvitakkaṁ …pe… uppannuppanne pāpake akusale dhamme nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti hanati byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti.
root_text.an4.114:8.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu hantā hoti.
root_text.an4.114:9.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu khamo hoti sītassa uṇhassa jighacchāya pipāsāya, ḍaṁsamakasavātātapasarīsapasamphassānaṁ duruttānaṁ durāgatānaṁ vacanapathānaṁ uppannānaṁ sārīrikānaṁ vedanānaṁ dukkhānaṁ tibbānaṁ kharānaṁ kaṭukānaṁ asātānaṁ amanāpānaṁ pāṇaharānaṁ adhivāsakajātiko hoti.
root_text.an4.114:9.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu khantā hoti.
root_text.an4.114:10.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yāyaṁ disā agatapubbā iminā dīghena addhunā yadidaṁ sabbasaṅkhārasamatho sabbūpadhipaṭinissaggo taṇhākkhayo virāgo nirodho nibbānaṁ, taṁ khippaññeva gantā hoti.
root_text.an4.114:10.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu gantā hoti.
root_text.an4.114:10.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu āhuneyyo hoti …pe… anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassā”ti.

translation_text.an4.114:1.1: “Mendicants, a royal bull elephant with four factors is worthy of a king, fit to serve a king, and is reckoned a factor of kingship.
translation_text.an4.114:1.3: A royal bull elephant listens, destroys, endures, and goes fast.
translation_text.an4.114:2.1: And how does a royal bull elephant listen?
translation_text.an4.114:2.2: It’s when a royal bull elephant pays attention, applies the mind, concentrates wholeheartedly, and actively listens to whatever task the elephant trainer has it do,
translation_text.an4.114:2.3: whether or not it has done it before.
translation_text.an4.114:2.5: That’s how a royal bull elephant listens.
translation_text.an4.114:3.1: And how does a royal bull elephant destroy?
translation_text.an4.114:3.2: It’s when a royal bull elephant in battle destroys elephants with their riders, horses with their riders, chariots and charioteers, and foot soldiers.
translation_text.an4.114:3.3: That’s how a royal bull elephant destroys.
translation_text.an4.114:4.1: And how does a royal bull elephant endure?
translation_text.an4.114:4.2: It’s when a royal bull elephant in battle endures being struck by spears, swords, arrows, and axes; it endures the thunder of the drums, kettledrums, horns, and cymbals.
translation_text.an4.114:4.3: That’s how a royal bull elephant endures.
translation_text.an4.114:5.1: And how does a royal bull elephant go fast?
translation_text.an4.114:5.2: It’s when a royal bull elephant swiftly goes in whatever direction the elephant trainer sends it,
translation_text.an4.114:5.3: whether or not it has been there before.
translation_text.an4.114:5.5: That’s how a royal bull elephant goes fast.
translation_text.an4.114:5.6: A royal bull elephant with four factors is worthy of a king, fit to serve a king, and is reckoned a factor of kingship.
translation_text.an4.114:6.1: In the same way, a mendicant with four qualities is worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of veneration with joined palms, and is the supreme field of merit for the world.
translation_text.an4.114:6.3: A mendicant listens, destroys, endures, and goes fast.
translation_text.an4.114:7.1: And how does a mendicant listen?
translation_text.an4.114:7.2: It’s when a mendicant pays attention, applies the mind, concentrates wholeheartedly, and actively listens when the teaching and training proclaimed by a Realized One is being taught.
translation_text.an4.114:8.1: And how does a mendicant destroy?
translation_text.an4.114:8.2: It’s when a mendicant doesn’t tolerate a sensual, malicious, or cruel thought. They don’t tolerate any bad, unskillful qualities that have arisen, but give them up, get rid of them, eliminate them, and obliterate them.
translation_text.an4.114:9.1: And how does a mendicant endure?
translation_text.an4.114:9.2: It’s when a mendicant endures cold, heat, hunger, and thirst; the touch of flies, mosquitoes, wind, sun, and reptiles; rude and unwelcome criticism; and they put up with physical pain—sharp, severe, acute, unpleasant, disagreeable, and life-threatening.
translation_text.an4.114:10.1: And how does a mendicant go fast?
translation_text.an4.114:10.2: It’s when a mendicant swiftly goes in the direction they’ve never gone before in all this long time; that is, the stilling of all activities, the letting go of all attachments, the ending of craving, fading away, cessation, extinguishment.
translation_text.an4.114:10.3: That’s how a mendicant goes fast.
translation_text.an4.114:10.4: A mendicant with these four qualities … is the supreme field of merit for the world.”

variant_text.an4.114:2.4: aṭṭhiṁ katvā → aṭṭhikatvā (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed) | sabbacetasā → sabbaṁ cetaso (sabbattha)
variant_text.an4.114:4.2: asippahārānaṁ usuppahārānaṁ → usuppahārānaṁ asippahārānaṁ (si ), pharasuppah idaṁ padaṁ sya-all potthakesu natthi
